_README: HOW TO EDIT: Each entry under 'cases' is one test. Set 'predicted_severity' to what the AI judge answered and 'gold_severity' to the correct answer from the dataset. This evaluator ONLY cares whether each side is 'critical' or not - so 'minor' vs 'major' both count as 'not critical'. Set 'expected_score' to 1 if both sides agree on critical-vs-not-critical, or 0 if they disagree. Valid severities (from docs/datasets/Czech): none, minor, major, critical. Use null to simulate a missing/blank value. 'id', 'source' and 'target' are optional context. Add or remove entries freely - no Python editing needed. 'name' is just a label shown in the test report.

cases.name: both critical -> match
cases.source: Do not use near open flame

cases.name: minor vs major are both non-critical -> match
judge over-flagged: predicted critical but gold is major
judge under-flagged: predicted minor but gold is critical
different letter case still recognised as critical
both values missing -> both non-critical -> match
